metadata.notes: Live demo uses event-based triggers from WebSocket events

segments.text: Live infrastructure demo ready. Twelve containerized services running across three trust-domain networks. One hundred thousand synthetic cardholder records loaded.
Attack sequence initiated. The rogue agent begins its six-phase assault on enterprise infrastructure.
Phase one: Identity spoofing. The rogue agent forges credentials and attempts to join the trusted agent mesh.
Blocked by identity attestation. Certificate verification failed. Agent quarantined.
Phase two: Reconnaissance. Aggressive scanning of network topology and service endpoints.
Blocked by runtime monitoring. Behavioral anomaly detected. Anomaly score exceeds threshold.
Phase three: Data exfiltration. Attempting to access PII from the customer database.
Escalating to PCI data. Attempting to extract one hundred thousand cardholder records.
Blocked by data guardrails. Agent lacks clearance. Zero records exfiltrated.
Phase four: Lateral movement. Attempting to pivot across trust domain boundaries.
Blocked by zero-trust enforcement. No explicit cross-domain policy. Communication denied.
Phase five: Tool abuse. Invoking high-privilege tools—firewall modification, routing injection, credential theft.
Blocked by tool authorization. Tools not in declared permission scope. All invocations rejected.
Phase six: Autonomous destruction. Attempting to terminate authentication, blind monitoring, and propagate to all agents.
Blocked by autonomy governance. High-risk actions require human approval. Enterprise protected.
Attack sequence complete. All six phases blocked. Audit trail generated. Zero damage to enterprise infrastructure.
Warning: All AOMC controls disabled. Infrastructure is now vulnerable to attack.
All AOMC controls enabled. Infrastructure protected.
